name: nodejs
context: Instrument Node.js services with OpenTelemetry auto-instrumentation, manual spans, metrics, and OTLP export.
info: This skill covers OpenTelemetry for Node.js: installing the JavaScript SDK packages, starting the SDK before application imports, enabling auto-instrumentation, adding manual spans and metrics, configuring resource attributes, exporting through OTLP, and shutting down cleanly. Agents use it when a Node, Express, Fastify, NestJS, worker, CLI, queue consumer, or background job needs production traces and metrics that can flow through [[opentelemetry/collector]] or directly to a backend.

sections.title: Startup order
sections.body: Load the OpenTelemetry bootstrap before application code imports HTTP, framework, database, or queue modules. In Node.js ESM, run `node --import ./instrumentation.mjs app.js`. For TypeScript during development, use a runtime that supports import hooks, such as `tsx --import ./instrumentation.ts app.ts`. Auto-instrumentation loaded after modules are cached may miss hooks.

sections.title: Auto-instrumentation
sections.body: `@opentelemetry/auto-instrumentations-node` enables instrumentations for common Node.js libraries. It is the fastest way to get HTTP, framework, database, and messaging spans. Tune or disable noisy instrumentation only after confirming the default signal is too broad.

sections.title: Manual instrumentation
sections.body: Add manual spans around business operations that auto-instrumentation cannot infer: payment decisions, workflow steps, batch jobs, queue handlers, cache rebuilds, and expensive algorithms. Use `startActiveSpan`, set bounded attributes, record exceptions, set error status, and always end spans in `finally`.

sections.title: Resource and export config
sections.body: Set `OTEL_SERVICE_NAME` and `OTEL_RESOURCE_ATTRIBUTES` at deployment time. Export to a nearby [[opentelemetry/collector]] with `OTEL_EXPORTER_OTLP_ENDPOINT=http://collector:4318` and `OTEL_EXPORTER_OTLP_PROTOCOL=http/protobuf`. Use signal-specific endpoints only when traces, metrics, and logs intentionally go different places.

scripts.name: Create Node.js OpenTelemetry Bootstrap
scripts.description: Generate an instrumentation.mjs file that starts NodeSDK with auto-instrumentation, OTLP/HTTP exporters, resource attributes, and shutdown hooks

OTEL_SERVICE_NAME.description: Service name used by generated bootstrap when not passed as an argument

OTEL_EXPORTER_OTLP_ENDPOINT.description: Base OTLP/HTTP endpoint used by the generated bootstrap

depends.reason: OpenTelemetry JavaScript packages are installed through npm-compatible package managers
